summary: The commenter emphasizes the importance of maintaining consistent cancer data reporting standards set by NAACCR for accurate population-based cancer surveillance. They express concern that the HTI-5 proposed deregulation could undermine these standards and support NAACCR's recommended revisions.
key_arguments: Population-based cancer surveillance relies on consistent, high-quality data standards.
NAACCR standards are critical and must be followed and updated regularly.
HTI-5 deregulation risks derailing existing cancer data reporting standards.
Maintaining standards is essential for sound public health policy and assessments.
recommendations: Reject or revise HTI-5 deregulatory measures that threaten NAACCR standards.
Ensure continued adherence to NAACCR data reporting standards in health IT certification.
notable_quote: The HTI-5 proposed deregulation measures risk the derailment of the existing NAACCR standards.
